titlestmt.titleproper: Witchcraft and possession-related movie posters, lobby cards, and other cinema memorabilia4781
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-06-15 18:33:40 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)
0.2 cubic feet added to extent to account for addition of Box 5; Box 5 added to guide

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Witchcraft and possession-related movie posters, lobby cards, and other cinema memorabilia
did.abstract: Movie posters and other advertisements for films involving witches, witchcraft, possession, devil worship and similar subjects.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Movie posters and other advertisements for films involving witches, witchcraft, possession, devil worship and similar subjects. The films are often of a "cult movie" nature from the United States and Europe with a few from Asia and Africa. Also includes other advertisements and mass market materials involving witches including two political T-shirts from 2010, one concerning the Delaware Tea Party (showing a witch stirring a cauldron-sized cup of tea) and one depicting Senate candidate Christine O'Donnell as a witch.

arrangement.head: COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
arrangement.p: Posters arranged chronologically then alphabetically by the original title of the film (according to IMDb) so that all the posters for the same film are kept together. Original title as well as other release titles also given.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Witchcraft and Possession-Related Movie Posters, Lobby Cards, and Other Cinema Memorabilia, #4781. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Magic
Witchcraft
Advertising -- Motion pictures -- United States.
Women in popular culture -- United States.
Political campaigns -- United States.
Political culture -- United States.
Witches in motion pictures.
Witchcraft -- Popular works.
Witches -- Pictorial works.
